prompt: I grabbed a screenshot of a competitor's pricing page and want to build something similar. It has 3 pricing tiers: Starter ($29/mo), Pro ($79/mo), and Enterprise (custom pricing). The Pro plan has a 'Most Popular' badge. Each card lists 5-6 feature bullets. There's a toggle at the top to switch between monthly and annual billing (annual saves 20%). The background is light gray, cards are white with subtle shadows. Code this up.

assertions.text: Output is a single valid HTML file (has <!DOCTYPE html> and closing </html>)
File contains a design rationale comment block documenting source material, detected niche, preserved elements, and improvements
All three tiers are present: Starter ($29), Pro ($79), and Enterprise
Pro plan has a 'Most Popular' badge or equivalent highlight
A monthly/annual billing toggle is present in the HTML
JavaScript code exists that updates prices when the billing toggle is switched (look for event listener on toggle + price update logic)
Annual pricing reflects the 20% discount (annual Starter ~$23, Pro ~$63)
Each pricing card contains a list of at least 4 feature bullet points
Tailwind CSS is loaded via CDN script tag
Page has at least title and meta description tags
